description_long: This object is a set of four color-coded recycling bins mounted on a black metal frame, designed for public waste separation. Each bin is a different color—blue, red, yellow, and green—corresponding to specific waste types: paper, plastic, metal, and glass, as indicated by large white recycling symbols and text on the front. The bins have domed, hinged lids that can be lifted for depositing waste, and the lids match the color of each bin. The bins are rectangular with slightly tapered sides and are suspended above the ground by the supporting frame, which has two sturdy legs for stability. The overall construction appears to be primarily plastic for the bins and lids, with metal used for the supporting frame. This setup is commonly found in urban parks, sidewalks, and public spaces to encourage proper waste sorting and recycling.
description: A set of four color-coded recycling bins with domed lids, mounted on a black metal frame, each labeled for a specific waste type. The bins are blue, red, yellow, and green, with clear recycling symbols and text on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four bins with their colored lids and clear recycling labels for paper, plastic, metal, and glass.
description_view_1: Side view showing the bins aligned in a row, highlighting the domed shape of the lids and the depth of the bins.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain backs of the bins and the supporting frame, with no visible labels or symbols.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the alignment and domed lids, with the blue bin in the foreground.
attribute_labeling_or_signage: Recycling symbols and waste type labels in white on the front of each bin
